topic: End-of-service calculation errors cost Oman businesses real money
post:
End-of-service gratuity disputes are the most expensive HR error in Oman.

Not because the amounts are always large. Because the Ministry of Labour takes them seriously, and the paper trail required to defend a calculation goes back years.

The formula is straightforward. The inputs aren't:
— Which salary figure applies (basic vs total)?
— How are unpaid leave days treated?
— What happens when someone changes from full-time to part-time mid-service?
— How are partial years calculated after year 3?

Most businesses calculate end-of-service manually, in a spreadsheet, at the moment someone resigns. Under time pressure. With incomplete records.

The employee gets a number. They disagree. HR recalculates. The number changes. The employee files a complaint. Now you're explaining a spreadsheet to a Ministry inspector.

A 15-year employee with a basic salary of OMR 600 — the difference between a correct and incorrect end-of-service calculation can exceed OMR 3,000. That's the dispute cost before you count legal fees or management time.

Automate the calculation from day one. Keep the audit trail from day one.

The cost of fixing a 5-year-old end-of-service error is always higher than the cost of getting it right on day one.

#OmanHR #WPS #EndOfService #SmartPROHub #HRCompliance
